auth_access_model: The public announcement page is open, but data endpoints require an API key or authorization token.

discovery_search.note: Query-string filtering and search exist, but all substantive data access is authenticated.

metadata_schema.note: The description endpoint and public docs help, but no full schema browser is wrapped.

entity_lookup.note: Location and description endpoints are available, but not a canonical identity layer.

identifier_crosswalks.note: Release, recovery, and location surfaces can support crosswalk work, but no explicit crosswalk workflow exists.

telemetry_passage.note: Telemetry and passage workflows are not part of RMIS.

hatchery.note: Release-oriented data can inform hatchery context, but no hatchery workflow is implemented.

harvest_fishery.note: Recovery and catch-sample endpoints expose fishery-relevant context.

genetics_gsi.note: Genetics and GSI workflows are not implemented.

provenance_versioning.note: The public announcement surface exposes the current RMIS version, but no richer provenance contract is modeled.

bulk_access_api_ergonomics.note: The API is machine-readable, but auth and reporting/API split surfaces add friction.

blockers: Authenticated access is required for useful data retrieval.
The current wrapper exposes only a narrow slice of the full API surface.
governance_constraints: The agent must treat RMIS as access-controlled rather than public-by-default.
